content.text:
[Image #1] In the past, I instructed some kind of task that the seeding for the initial house should be a proper house, like a really well-structured house. And what we currently have is just a mess, honestly. So what I want you to look into is... Seed a really concrete good looking house use the internal logic whatever i don't know but i want to see a proper house not the weird stuff that you see in my image which is just like random walls random doors and so on think about how a good structure would actually look like and then i want that as the seating and what we currently have is just not good so Yeah, please look into this.

It might be that it's in the S3 bucket on the database. I don't know. But the current house that we have as the example house is just terrible.

attachment.content:
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

input.prompt:
Search for seed data files in this BIM AI project at /Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai. I'm looking for:
1. Files named "seed", "fixture", "demo", "example" or similar
2. Files that define initial house/building data (walls, doors, rooms)
3. Database seed scripts or migration files with initial data
4. S3 upload scripts or JSON files with project data
5. Any scripts that create a default/example project

Look in all directories including backend, api, scripts, db, database, fixtures, etc.

Report the full file paths and a brief description of what each file contains. Be thorough - search breadth: very thorough.

content.text:
Perfect! Now I have a comprehensive picture. Let me create a final summary document with all the findings:

## Summary of Seed Data Files in BIM AI Project

I found a comprehensive set of seed data files and fixture systems. Here's the complete breakdown:

### **1. Main Database Seed Script**
- **File**: `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/app/scripts/seed.py`
- **Description**: Python script that initializes the database with a demo project named "BIM AI demo project". Creates:
  - A demo project (UUID-based deterministic IDs)
  - A demo model with revision 1
  - A demo document containing walls, doors, windows, rooms, levels, grid lines, dimensions, viewpoints, and an issue element
  - Seed comment with initial guidance ("Try Plan ( toolbar ) · Snap + tools...")
  - Uses SQLAlchemy async with PostgreSQL backend
  - Defines initial model structure with 2 levels (Ground, Upper), walls, doors, windows, and a room

### **2. Seed House V2 Commands (Comprehensive Fixture)**
- **File**: `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/packages/cli/lib/seed-house-v2-commands.mjs`
- **Description**: JavaScript module defining 810 lines of commands to build a complete 2-storey residential fixture house
- **Elements created**: 
  - 3 levels (Ground, Upper, Roof Apex)
  - 1 site pad with setback
  - 16 walls (6 exterior ground + 4 interior ground + 4 exterior upper + 2 gable)
  - 3 floors/slabs (ground L-shape, upper with cantilever, terrace)
  - 1 pitched roof
  - 1 stair (17 risers × 176mm)
  - 3 railings (stair, balcony, terrace)
  - 5 doors
  - 7 windows
  - 9 rooms (5 ground + 4 upper)
  - 2 section cuts (A–A, B–B)
  - 2 plan views
  - 3 3D viewpoints
  - 1 sheet with 4 viewports
  - 5 schedules (doors, windows, rooms, wall types, materials)
- **All IDs prefixed**: `seed-` for easy cleanup/reset per spec §27.3

### **3. One-Family-Home Commands**
- **File**: `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/packages/cli/lib/one-family-home-commands.mjs`
- **Description**: 784-line fixture creating a canonical single-storey residential house used by CLI and agents
- **Purpose**: Simpler reference model for demos, replay determinism, and visual regression baselines
- **Elements**: Ground level, walls, stair, upper slab, roof slice, documentation scaffolding

### **4. One-Family-Home Apply Script**
- **File**: `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/scripts/apply-one-family-home.mjs`
- **Description**: Node.js script that rebuilds the demo model by:
  1. Fetching current snapshot via API
  2. Deleting ALL current element IDs
  3. Applying fixed one-family-home command list via bundle endpoint
- **Key constraint**: Uses dynamic deletion instead of static JSON to avoid snapshot drift

### **5. Fixture JSON Data Files**
- **File**: `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/app/tests/fixtures/golden_exchange_snapshot.json`
- **Description**: JSON snapshot fixture with complete building model including:
  - 2 levels (EG, OG)
  - Walls, doors, windows, floors, slab openings, rooms
  - Roof, stair, plan views, section cuts, sheets, schedules
  - Full exchange documentation elements
  - Used by test suite for deterministic testing

### **6. Example House Briefs**
- **File**: `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/spec/examples/small-house-brief.json`
- **Description**: Minimal brief schema (version 1) for house generation with:
  - Site dimensions (14m × 10m)
  - 1 floor
  - Room list with target areas (Living, Bedroom, Bath)
  - Input to `bim-ai plan-house` command

- **File**: `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/spec/examples/small-house-bundle.json`
- **Description**: Placeholder bundle structure (commands array) for validated commands from snapshot IDs

### **7. Fixture Documentation**
- **File**: `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/spec/fixtures/cli-one-family-home.md`
- **Description**: Markdown guide for the CLI one-family-home fixture:
  - Instructions for applying the reference house
  - Geometry notes on wall coordinates
  - Browser validation checklist
  - Model ID: `75cd3d5c-f28c-5dd2-b8bf-8cbba71fd10f` (deterministic)

### **8. Test Files Using Seed Data**
- **File**: `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/app/tests/test_seed_house_v2_bundle.py`
  - Tests that all element IDs use `seed-` prefix
  - Validates all required element kinds are present
  - Verifies element counts match spec §27 (levels=3, walls=16, rooms=9, etc.)

- **File**: `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/app/tests/test_golden_exchange_fixture.py`
  - Loads and validates golden snapshot JSON
  - Tests exchange documentation kinds presence
  - Validates IFC geometry handling
  - Tests section projections and schedule derivation

### **9. House Generation Protocol**
- **File**: `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/spec/house-generation-protocol.md`
- **Description**: Agent loop documentation for house generation:
  - Brief schema specification
  - Input files (version, stylePreset, siteWidthM, siteDepthM, floors, rooms)
  - Command validation flow
  - Evidence capture endpoints

### **10. Database Configuration**
- **File**: `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/.env.example`
- **Description**: Environment variables for seed configuration:
  - PostgreSQL connection string
  - Redis URL for state management
  - S3 endpoint (for optional exports)
  - Design system defaults

### **11. Database Tables**
- **File**: `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/app/bim_ai/tables.py`
- **Data structures**:
  - `ProjectRecord`: Stores projects with id, slug, title
  - `ModelRecord`: Stores models linked to projects with document JSON
  - `CommentRecord`: Stores comments with user info, element references, coordinates
  - `UndoStackRecord` / `RedoStackRecord`: For command history

### **Key Design Patterns**
1. **Deterministic UUIDs**: Use `uuid.uuid5()` with namespace URL for reproducible IDs
2. **Prefix Convention**: Seed elements use `seed-` prefix for easy reset
3. **Dynamic Deletion**: Scripts delete ALL current IDs from snapshots instead of static lists (avoids drift)
4. **Bundle API**: Commands committed via `/api/models/{id}/commands/bundle` endpoint
5. **Async SQLAlchemy**: PostgreSQL backend with async support via asyncpg

All seed data is designed to be fully deterministic and reproducible, supporting automated testing, visual regression baselines, and agent replay scenarios.

1	#!/usr/bin/env python3
2	from __future__ import annotations
3	
4	import asyncio
5	import uuid
6	from datetime import UTC, datetime
7	
8	from bim_ai.db import SessionMaker, init_db_schema
9	from bim_ai.document import Document
10	from bim_ai.elements import (
11	    CameraMm,
12	    DimensionElem,
13	    DoorElem,
14	    Element,
15	    GridLineElem,
16	    IssueElem,
17	    LevelElem,
18	    RoomElem,
19	    Vec2Mm,
20	    Vec3Mm,
21	    ViewpointElem,
22	    WallElem,
23	    WindowElem,
24	)
25	from bim_ai.engine import try_commit_bundle
26	from bim_ai.tables import CommentRecord, ModelRecord, ProjectRecord
27	
28	PROJECT_ID = uuid.uuid5(uuid.NAMESPACE_URL, "bim-ai:project:demo")
29	MODEL_ID = uuid.uuid5(uuid.NAMESPACE_URL, "bim-ai:model:demo-main")
30	COMMENT_ID = uuid.uuid5(uuid.NAMESPACE_URL, "bim-ai:comment:demo-1")
31	
32	
33	def demo_document() -> Document:
34	    wall_spine = "wall-001"
35	    wall_ns = "wall-002"
36	
37	    elems: dict[str, Element] = {
38	        "lvl-1": LevelElem(kind="level", id="lvl-1", name="Ground", elevationMm=0),
39	        "lvl-2": LevelElem(kind="level", id="lvl-2", name="Upper", elevationMm=3000),
40	        wall_spine: WallElem(
41	            kind="wall",
42	            id=wall_spine,
43	            name="Spine wall",
44	            levelId="lvl-1",
45	            start=Vec2Mm(xMm=0, yMm=0),
46	            end=Vec2Mm(xMm=14000, yMm=0),
47	            thicknessMm=200,
48	            heightMm=2800,
49	        ),
50	        wall_ns: WallElem(
51	            kind="wall",
52	            id=wall_ns,
53	            name="North façade",
54	            levelId="lvl-1",
55	            start=Vec2Mm(xMm=0, yMm=-4000),
56	            end=Vec2Mm(xMm=14000, yMm=-4000),
57	            thicknessMm=200,
58	            heightMm=2800,
59	        ),
60	        "wall-020": WallElem(
61	            kind="wall",
62	            id="wall-020",
63	            name="West end",
64	            levelId="lvl-1",
65	            start=Vec2Mm(xMm=0, yMm=-4000),
66	            end=Vec2Mm(xMm=0, yMm=2000),
67	            thicknessMm=200,
68	            heightMm=2800,
69	        ),
70	        "wall-up-1": WallElem(
71	            kind="wall",
72	            id="wall-up-1",
73	            name="Upper spine",
74	            levelId="lvl-2",
75	            start=Vec2Mm(xMm=2000, yMm=-2000),
76	            end=Vec2Mm(xMm=9000, yMm=-2000),
77	            thicknessMm=200,
78	            heightMm=2800,
79	        ),
80	        "door-001": DoorElem(
81	            kind="door",
82	            id="door-001",
83	            name="Entry",
84	            wallId=wall_spine,
85	            alongT=0.25,
86	            widthMm=900,
87	        ),
88	        "win-001": WindowElem(
89	            kind="window",
90	            id="win-001",
91	            name="Ribbon window",
92	            wallId=wall_ns,
93	            alongT=0.45,
94	            widthMm=2200,
95	            sillHeightMm=900,
96	            heightMm=1600,
97	        ),
98	        "room-001": RoomElem(
99	            kind="room",
100	            id="room-001",
101	            name="Open office",
102	            levelId="lvl-1",
103	            outlineMm=[
104	                Vec2Mm(xMm=3000, yMm=-3000),
105	                Vec2Mm(xMm=11000, yMm=-3000),
106	                Vec2Mm(xMm=11000, yMm=-500),
107	                Vec2Mm(xMm=3000, yMm=-500),
108	            ],
109	        ),
110	        "grid-a": GridLineElem(
111	            kind="grid_line",
112	            id="grid-a",
113	            name="Axis A",
114	            label="A",
115	            levelId=None,
116	            start=Vec2Mm(xMm=-2000, yMm=-5000),
117	            end=Vec2Mm(xMm=16000, yMm=-5000),
118	        ),
119	        "grid-b": GridLineElem(
120	            kind="grid_line",
121	            id="grid-b",
122	            name="Axis B",
123	            label="B",
124	            levelId=None,
125	            start=Vec2Mm(xMm=-2000, yMm=-2000),
126	            end=Vec2Mm(xMm=16000, yMm=-2000),
127	        ),
128	        "grid-1": GridLineElem(
129	            kind="grid_line",
130	            id="grid-1",
131	            name="Line 1",
132	            label="1",
133	            levelId=None,
134	            start=Vec2Mm(xMm=14000, yMm=-6000),
135	            end=Vec2Mm(xMm=14000, yMm=2500),
136	        ),
137	        "dim-001": DimensionElem(
138	            kind="dimension",
139	            id="dim-001",
140	            name="Width check",
141	            levelId="lvl-1",
142	            aMm=Vec2Mm(xMm=0, yMm=0),
143	            bMm=Vec2Mm(xMm=8000, yMm=0),
144	            offsetMm=Vec2Mm(xMm=0, yMm=1200),
145	        ),
146	        "vp-001": ViewpointElem(
147	            kind="viewpoint",
148	            id="vp-001",
149	            name="Default orbit",
150	            camera=CameraMm(
151	                position=Vec3Mm(xMm=9500, yMm=-7500, zMm=6500),
152	                target=Vec3Mm(xMm=4000, yMm=-1500, zMm=0),
153	                up=Vec3Mm(xMm=0, yMm=0, zMm=1),
154	            ),
155	            mode="orbit_3d",
156	        ),
157	        "issue-001": IssueElem(
158	            kind="issue",
159	            id="issue-001",
160	            title="Starter issue — coordination kickoff",
161	            status="open",
162	            elementIds=[wall_spine, "door-001"],
163	            viewpointId="vp-001",
164	        ),
165	    }
166	
167	    return Document(revision=1, elements=elems)
168	
169	
170	def document_wire(doc: Document) -> dict:
171	    return {
172	        "revision": doc.revision,
173	        "elements": {kid: elem.model_dump(by_alias=True) for kid, elem in doc.elements.items()},
174	    }
175	
176	
177	async def seed_async() -> None:
178	    await init_db_schema()
179	    demo = demo_document()
180	    ok_bundle, demo2, _, _, _code = try_commit_bundle(
181	        demo,
182	        [
183	            {
184	                "type": "createRoomRectangle",
185	                "levelId": "lvl-1",
186	                "origin": {"xMm": 15500, "yMm": -5500},
187	                "widthMm": 2600,
188	                "depthMm": 1900,
189	            }
190	        ],
191	    )
192	    if ok_bundle:
193	        demo = demo2
194	    demo_wire = document_wire(demo)
195	    now = datetime.now(UTC)
196	
197	    async with SessionMaker() as session:
198	        if await session.get(ProjectRecord, PROJECT_ID) is None:
199	            session.add(ProjectRecord(id=PROJECT_ID, slug="demo", title="BIM AI demo project"))
200	
201	        row = await session.get(ModelRecord, MODEL_ID)
202	        if row is None:
203	            session.add(
204	                ModelRecord(
205	                    id=MODEL_ID,
206	                    project_id=PROJECT_ID,
207	                    slug="main",
208	                    revision=demo.revision,
209	                    document=demo_wire,
210	                ),
211	            )
212	        else:
213	            row.document = demo_wire
214	            row.revision = demo.revision
215	            row.slug = "main"
216	            row.project_id = PROJECT_ID
217	
218	        crowd = await session.get(CommentRecord, COMMENT_ID)
219	        if crowd is None:
220	            session.add(
221	                CommentRecord(
222	                    id=COMMENT_ID,
223	                    model_id=MODEL_ID,
224	                    user_display="Seed bot",

file.content:
#!/usr/bin/env python3
from __future__ import annotations

import asyncio
import uuid
from datetime import UTC, datetime

from bim_ai.db import SessionMaker, init_db_schema
from bim_ai.document import Document
from bim_ai.elements import (
    CameraMm,
    DimensionElem,
    DoorElem,
    Element,
    GridLineElem,
    IssueElem,
    LevelElem,
    RoomElem,
    Vec2Mm,
    Vec3Mm,
    ViewpointElem,
    WallElem,
    WindowElem,
)
from bim_ai.engine import try_commit_bundle
from bim_ai.tables import CommentRecord, ModelRecord, ProjectRecord

PROJECT_ID = uuid.uuid5(uuid.NAMESPACE_URL, "bim-ai:project:demo")
MODEL_ID = uuid.uuid5(uuid.NAMESPACE_URL, "bim-ai:model:demo-main")
COMMENT_ID = uuid.uuid5(uuid.NAMESPACE_URL, "bim-ai:comment:demo-1")


def demo_document() -> Document:
    wall_spine = "wall-001"
    wall_ns = "wall-002"

    elems: dict[str, Element] = {
        "lvl-1": LevelElem(kind="level", id="lvl-1", name="Ground", elevationMm=0),
        "lvl-2": LevelElem(kind="level", id="lvl-2", name="Upper", elevationMm=3000),
        wall_spine: WallElem(
            kind="wall",
            id=wall_spine,
            name="Spine wall",
            levelId="lvl-1",
            start=Vec2Mm(xMm=0, yMm=0),
            end=Vec2Mm(xMm=14000, yMm=0),
            thicknessMm=200,
            heightMm=2800,
        ),
        wall_ns: WallElem(
            kind="wall",
            id=wall_ns,
            name="North façade",
            levelId="lvl-1",
            start=Vec2Mm(xMm=0, yMm=-4000),
            end=Vec2Mm(xMm=14000, yMm=-4000),
            thicknessMm=200,
            heightMm=2800,
        ),
        "wall-020": WallElem(
            kind="wall",
            id="wall-020",
            name="West end",
            levelId="lvl-1",
            start=Vec2Mm(xMm=0, yMm=-4000),
            end=Vec2Mm(xMm=0, yMm=2000),
            thicknessMm=200,
            heightMm=2800,
        ),
        "wall-up-1": WallElem(
            kind="wall",
            id="wall-up-1",
            name="Upper spine",
            levelId="lvl-2",
            start=Vec2Mm(xMm=2000, yMm=-2000),
            end=Vec2Mm(xMm=9000, yMm=-2000),
            thicknessMm=200,
            heightMm=2800,
        ),
        "door-001": DoorElem(
            kind="door",
            id="door-001",
            name="Entry",
            wallId=wall_spine,
            alongT=0.25,
            widthMm=900,
        ),
        "win-001": WindowElem(
            kind="window",
            id="win-001",
            name="Ribbon window",
            wallId=wall_ns,
            alongT=0.45,
            widthMm=2200,
            sillHeightMm=900,
            heightMm=1600,
        ),
        "room-001": RoomElem(
            kind="room",
            id="room-001",
            name="Open office",
            levelId="lvl-1",
            outlineMm=[
                Vec2Mm(xMm=3000, yMm=-3000),
                Vec2Mm(xMm=11000, yMm=-3000),
                Vec2Mm(xMm=11000, yMm=-500),
                Vec2Mm(xMm=3000, yMm=-500),
            ],
        ),
        "grid-a": GridLineElem(
            kind="grid_line",
            id="grid-a",
            name="Axis A",
            label="A",
            levelId=None,
            start=Vec2Mm(xMm=-2000, yMm=-5000),
            end=Vec2Mm(xMm=16000, yMm=-5000),
        ),
        "grid-b": GridLineElem(
            kind="grid_line",
            id="grid-b",
            name="Axis B",
            label="B",
            levelId=None,
            start=Vec2Mm(xMm=-2000, yMm=-2000),
            end=Vec2Mm(xMm=16000, yMm=-2000),
        ),
        "grid-1": GridLineElem(
            kind="grid_line",
            id="grid-1",
            name="Line 1",
            label="1",
            levelId=None,
            start=Vec2Mm(xMm=14000, yMm=-6000),
            end=Vec2Mm(xMm=14000, yMm=2500),
        ),
        "dim-001": DimensionElem(
            kind="dimension",
            id="dim-001",
            name="Width check",
            levelId="lvl-1",
            aMm=Vec2Mm(xMm=0, yMm=0),
            bMm=Vec2Mm(xMm=8000, yMm=0),
            offsetMm=Vec2Mm(xMm=0, yMm=1200),
        ),
        "vp-001": ViewpointElem(
            kind="viewpoint",
            id="vp-001",
            name="Default orbit",
            camera=CameraMm(
                position=Vec3Mm(xMm=9500, yMm=-7500, zMm=6500),
                target=Vec3Mm(xMm=4000, yMm=-1500, zMm=0),
                up=Vec3Mm(xMm=0, yMm=0, zMm=1),
            ),
            mode="orbit_3d",
        ),
        "issue-001": IssueElem(
            kind="issue",
            id="issue-001",
            title="Starter issue — coordination kickoff",
            status="open",
            elementIds=[wall_spine, "door-001"],
            viewpointId="vp-001",
        ),
    }

    return Document(revision=1, elements=elems)


def document_wire(doc: Document) -> dict:
    return {
        "revision": doc.revision,
        "elements": {kid: elem.model_dump(by_alias=True) for kid, elem in doc.elements.items()},
    }


async def seed_async() -> None:
    await init_db_schema()
    demo = demo_document()
    ok_bundle, demo2, _, _, _code = try_commit_bundle(
        demo,
        [
            {
                "type": "createRoomRectangle",
                "levelId": "lvl-1",
                "origin": {"xMm": 15500, "yMm": -5500},
                "widthMm": 2600,
                "depthMm": 1900,
            }
        ],
    )
    if ok_bundle:
        demo = demo2
    demo_wire = document_wire(demo)
    now = datetime.now(UTC)

    async with SessionMaker() as session:
        if await session.get(ProjectRecord, PROJECT_ID) is None:
            session.add(ProjectRecord(id=PROJECT_ID, slug="demo", title="BIM AI demo project"))

        row = await session.get(ModelRecord, MODEL_ID)
        if row is None:
            session.add(
                ModelRecord(
                    id=MODEL_ID,
                    project_id=PROJECT_ID,
                    slug="main",
                    revision=demo.revision,
                    document=demo_wire,
                ),
            )
        else:
            row.document = demo_wire
            row.revision = demo.revision
            row.slug = "main"
            row.project_id = PROJECT_ID

        crowd = await session.get(CommentRecord, COMMENT_ID)
        if crowd is None:
            session.add(
                CommentRecord(
                    id=COMMENT_ID,
                    model_id=MODEL_ID,
                    user_display="Seed bot",
                    body="Try Plan ( toolbar ) · Snap + tools: Wall / Door / Window / Room / Grid / Dimension.",
                    element_id=None,
                    level_id="lvl-1",
                    anchor_x_mm=6200,
                    anchor_y_mm=-2800,
                    resolved=False,
                    created_at=now,
                    updated_at=now,
                ),
            )

        await session.commit()

    print("seed: OK — demo/main model:", str(MODEL_ID))


def main() -> None:
    asyncio.run(seed_async())


if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()

/** @typedef {Record<string, unknown>} BimCommand */

/** Closed CCW footing outline (aligned to wall spine grid from the canonical one-family footprint). */
const EG_OUTLINE_MM = [
  { xMm: 5000, yMm: 4000 },
  { xMm: 17000, yMm: 4000 },
  { xMm: 17000, yMm: 12400 },
  { xMm: 5000, yMm: 12400 },
];

/** Stair shaft through EG + OG slabs (aligned to main hall run east–west). */
const STAIR_SHAFT_MM = [
  { xMm: 10280, yMm: 7800 },
  { xMm: 14920, yMm: 7800 },
  { xMm: 14920, yMm: 8900 },
  { xMm: 10280, yMm: 8900 },
];

/**
 * Canonical one-family footprint + two-storey “golden” scaffolding used by CLI `plan-house`,
 * `scripts/apply-one-family-home.mjs`, and agent regressions.
 * Geometry follows the original single-storey sleeper + spine junction (T-aligned).
 * Adds Upper level slab, EG/OG floors, slab openings, stair, roof slice, façade duplicate on Upper,
 * documentation scaffolding (templates, schedules, sections, sheets), sample family/type assignments,
 * and extra viewpoints suitable for Evidence / screenshot baselines.
 * @returns {BimCommand[]}
 */
export function buildOneFamilyHomeCommands() {
  return [
    { type: 'createLevel', id: 'hf-lvl-1', name: 'Ground', elevationMm: 0 },

    {
      type: 'createWall',
      id: 'hf-w-so',
      name: 'South facade',
      levelId: 'hf-lvl-1',
      start: { xMm: 5000, yMm: 4000 },
      end: { xMm: 17000, yMm: 4000 },
      thicknessMm: 200,
      heightMm: 2800,
    },
    {
      type: 'createWall',
      id: 'hf-w-ea',
      name: 'East facade',
      levelId: 'hf-lvl-1',
      start: { xMm: 17000, yMm: 4000 },
      end: { xMm: 17000, yMm: 12400 },
      thicknessMm: 200,
      heightMm: 2800,
    },
    {
      type: 'createWall',
      id: 'hf-w-no',
      name: 'North facade',
      levelId: 'hf-lvl-1',
      start: { xMm: 17000, yMm: 12400 },
      end: { xMm: 5000, yMm: 12400 },
      thicknessMm: 200,
      heightMm: 2800,
    },
    {
      type: 'createWall',
      id: 'hf-w-we',
      name: 'West facade',
      levelId: 'hf-lvl-1',
      start: { xMm: 5000, yMm: 12400 },
      end: { xMm: 5000, yMm: 4000 },
      thicknessMm: 200,
      heightMm: 2800,
    },

    {
      type: 'createWall',
      id: 'hf-w-spine',
      name: 'Main spine hall',
      levelId: 'hf-lvl-1',
      start: { xMm: 11000, yMm: 4180 },
      end: { xMm: 11000, yMm: 12220 },
      thicknessMm: 200,
      heightMm: 2800,
    },

    {
      type: 'createWall',
      id: 'hf-w-split-w',
      name: 'West sleeper',
      levelId: 'hf-lvl-1',
      start: { xMm: 5220, yMm: 8150 },
      end: { xMm: 10900, yMm: 8150 },
      thicknessMm: 200,
      heightMm: 2800,
    },
    {
      type: 'createWall',
      id: 'hf-w-split-e',
      name: 'East sleeper',
      levelId: 'hf-lvl-1',
      start: { xMm: 11100, yMm: 8150 },
      end: { xMm: 16780, yMm: 8150 },
      thicknessMm: 200,
      heightMm: 2800,
    },

    {
      type: 'createWall',
      id: 'hf-w-bath-ns',
      name: 'Bath partition',
      levelId: 'hf-lvl-1',
      start: { xMm: 7400, yMm: 4180 },
      end: { xMm: 7400, yMm: 8040 },
      thicknessMm: 150,
      heightMm: 2800,
    },
    {
      type: 'createWall',
      id: 'hf-w-bath-ew',
      name: 'Bath head wall',
      levelId: 'hf-lvl-1',
      start: { xMm: 5280, yMm: 8040 },
      end: { xMm: 7260, yMm: 8040 },
      thicknessMm: 150,
      heightMm: 2800,
    },

    {
      type: 'createWall',
      id: 'hf-w-bed2-ns',
      name: 'NE bedroom split',
      levelId: 'hf-lvl-1',
      start: { xMm: 14200, yMm: 8360 },
      end: { xMm: 14200, yMm: 12180 },
      thicknessMm: 200,
      heightMm: 2800,
    },

    /** Upper datum for second-storey slab — 2800 storey height typical for residential demos. */
    { type: 'createLevel', id: 'hf-lvl-2', name: 'Upper', elevationMm: 2800 },

    {
      type: 'createFloor',
      id: 'hf-fl-eg',
      name: 'EG structural slab',
      levelId: 'hf-lvl-1',
      boundaryMm: EG_OUTLINE_MM,
      thicknessMm: 220,
      structureThicknessMm: 160,
      finishThicknessMm: 40,
      roomBounded: false,
    },

    {
      type: 'createSlabOpening',
      id: 'hf-opening-stair-eg',
      name: 'Stair shaft (EG slab)',
      hostFloorId: 'hf-fl-eg',
      boundaryMm: STAIR_SHAFT_MM,
      isShaft: true,
    },

    /** Upper shell uses the same façade lines (stacked construction). Open hall + loft/studio programmatic rooms. */
    {
      type: 'createWall',
      id: 'hf-u-w-so',
      name: 'Upper south facade',
      levelId: 'hf-lvl-2',
      start: { xMm: 5000, yMm: 4000 },
      end: { xMm: 17000, yMm: 4000 },
      thicknessMm: 200,
      heightMm: 2600,
    },
    {
      type: 'createWall',
      id: 'hf-u-w-ea',
      name: 'Upper east facade',
      levelId: 'hf-lvl-2',
      start: { xMm: 17000, yMm: 4000 },
      end: { xMm: 17000, yMm: 12400 },
      thicknessMm: 200,
      heightMm: 2600,
    },
    {
      type: 'createWall',
      id: 'hf-u-w-no',
      name: 'Upper north facade',
      levelId: 'hf-lvl-2',
      start: { xMm: 17000, yMm: 12400 },
      end: { xMm: 5000, yMm: 12400 },
      thicknessMm: 200,
      heightMm: 2600,
    },
    {
      type: 'createWall',
      id: 'hf-u-w-we',
      name: 'Upper west facade',
      levelId: 'hf-lvl-2',
      start: { xMm: 5000, yMm: 12400 },
      end: { xMm: 5000, yMm: 4000 },
      thicknessMm: 200,
      heightMm: 2600,
    },

    /** Light partition zoning on Upper near previous NE split line. */
    {
      type: 'createWall',
      id: 'hf-u-w-split-ns',
      name: 'Upper studio split',
      levelId: 'hf-lvl-2',
      start: { xMm: 13800, yMm: 8600 },
      end: { xMm: 13800, yMm: 12080 },
      thicknessMm: 150,
      heightMm: 2600,
    },

    {
      type: 'createFloor',
      id: 'hf-fl-og',
      name: 'OG structural slab',
      levelId: 'hf-lvl-2',
      boundaryMm: EG_OUTLINE_MM,
      thicknessMm: 220,
      structureThicknessMm: 160,
      finishThicknessMm: 40,
      roomBounded: false,
    },

    {
      type: 'createSlabOpening',
      id: 'hf-opening-stair-og',
      name: 'Stair shaft (OG slab)',
      hostFloorId: 'hf-fl-og',
      boundaryMm: STAIR_SHAFT_MM,
      isShaft: true,
    },

    /** Run horizontally across corridor with ~15×275 mm treads for 2800 mm vertical rise ×175 mm risers. */
    {
      type: 'createStair',
      id: 'hf-stair-main',
      name: 'Hall stair',
      baseLevelId: 'hf-lvl-1',
      topLevelId: 'hf-lvl-2',
      runStartMm: { xMm: 10400, yMm: 8350 },
      runEndMm: { xMm: 14700, yMm: 8350 },
      widthMm: 1050,
      riserMm: 175,
      treadMm: 275,
    },

    {
      type: 'createRailing',
      id: 'hf-rail-stair-s',
      name: 'Stair guardrail south',
      hostedStairId: 'hf-stair-main',
      pathMm: [
        { xMm: 10350, yMm: 7830 },
        { xMm: 14870, yMm: 7830 },
      ],
    },

    /** Reference roof modeled as simple footprint prism on Upper datum (production roof solver out of MVP scope). */
    {
      type: 'createRoof',
      id: 'hf-roof-main',
      name: 'Hip roof prism (demo)',
      referenceLevelId: 'hf-lvl-2',
      footprintMm: EG_OUTLINE_MM,
      overhangMm: 450,
      slopeDeg: 32,
    },

    {
      type: 'createRoomOutline',
      id: 'hf-room-bath',
      name: 'Bath',
      levelId: 'hf-lvl-1',
      outlineMm: [
        { xMm: 5320, yMm: 4320 },
        { xMm: 7180, yMm: 4320 },
        { xMm: 7180, yMm: 7860 },
        { xMm: 5320, yMm: 7860 },
      ],
    },
    {
      type: 'createRoomOutline',
      id: 'hf-room-kitchen',
      name: 'Kitchen / dining',
      levelId: 'hf-lvl-1',
      outlineMm: [
        { xMm: 7560, yMm: 4320 },
        { xMm: 10680, yMm: 4320 },
        { xMm: 10680, yMm: 7920 },
        { xMm: 7560, yMm: 7920 },
      ],
    },
    {
      type: 'createRoomOutline',
      id: 'hf-room-bed3',
      name: 'Bedroom west',
      levelId: 'hf-lvl-1',
      outlineMm: [
        { xMm: 5280, yMm: 8420 },
        { xMm: 10680, yMm: 8420 },
        { xMm: 10680, yMm: 12200 },
        { xMm: 5280, yMm: 12200 },
      ],
    },
    {
      type: 'createRoomOutline',
      id: 'hf-room-master',
      name: 'Master bedroom',
      levelId: 'hf-lvl-1',
      outlineMm: [
        { xMm: 11320, yMm: 4320 },
        { xMm: 16680, yMm: 4320 },
        { xMm: 16680, yMm: 7920 },
        { xMm: 11320, yMm: 7920 },
      ],
    },
    {
      type: 'createRoomOutline',
      id: 'hf-room-living',
      name: 'Living',
      levelId: 'hf-lvl-1',
      outlineMm: [
        { xMm: 11320, yMm: 8480 },
        { xMm: 14040, yMm: 8480 },
        { xMm: 14040, yMm: 12120 },
        { xMm: 11320, yMm: 12120 },
      ],
    },
    {
      type: 'createRoomOutline',
      id: 'hf-room-bed2',
      name: 'Bedroom NE',
      levelId: 'hf-lvl-1',
      outlineMm: [
        { xMm: 14480, yMm: 8560 },
        { xMm: 16680, yMm: 8560 },
        { xMm: 16680, yMm: 12120 },
        { xMm: 14480, yMm: 12120 },
      ],
    },

    /** Upper storey rooms (“loft” zoning). */
    {
      type: 'createRoomOutline',
      id: 'hf-room-u-loft',
      name: 'Loft west',
      levelId: 'hf-lvl-2',
      outlineMm: [
        { xMm: 5380, yMm: 8680 },
        { xMm: 10680, yMm: 8680 },
        { xMm: 10680, yMm: 12080 },
        { xMm: 5380, yMm: 12080 },
      ],
    },
    {
      type: 'createRoomOutline',
      id: 'hf-room-u-studio',
      name: 'Studio east',
      levelId: 'hf-lvl-2',
      outlineMm: [
        { xMm: 11200, yMm: 8680 },

    {

      viewportsMm: [
        {

        {

    {

    {

    {

    {

content.content:
1	/** @typedef {Record<string, unknown>} BimCommand */
2	
3	/**
4	 * Seed house V2 — fixture spec spec/ui-ux-redesign-v1-spec.md §27.
5	 *
6	 * Reference image silhouette: a 2-storey gable house with a cantilevered
7	 * upper-floor balcony to the south and a ground-level terrace pad. The
8	 * fixture exercises every primary element class so demos, replay
9	 * determinism, and visual-regression baselines can be anchored on a
10	 * single document.
11	 *
12	 * All element ids use the `seed-` prefix per §27.3 to make reset trivial:
13	 *   Document.removeWhereIdStartsWith("seed-")
14	 *
15	 * Coordinate frame: planar mm, +X east, +Y north (canvas convention).
16	 *
17	 * L-shape ground footprint (CCW outer ring):
18	 *   (5000, 4000) → (17000, 4000)            // south of main
19	 *   (17000, 4000) → (17000, 8000)           // east of main below wing
20	 *   (17000, 8000) → (21000, 8000)           // south of utility wing
21	 *   (21000, 8000) → (21000, 12000)          // east of utility wing
22	 *   (21000, 12000) → (5000, 12000)          // north top
23	 *   (5000, 12000) → (5000, 4000)            // west
24	 *
25	 * Upper rectangle (set back 1 m on the south to form the cantilevered
26	 * balcony floor):
27	 *   (5000, 5000) — (17000, 12000)           // 12 m × 7 m
28	 *
29	 * Terrace pad (12 m × 9 m, -50 mm):
30	 *   (5000, -5000) — (17000, 4000)
31	 *
32	 * @returns {BimCommand[]}
33	 */
34	export function buildSeedHouseV2Commands() {
35	  const cmds = [];
36	
37	  // ── 1. Levels (3) ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────
38	  cmds.push(
39	    { type: 'createLevel', id: 'seed-lvl-ground', name: 'Ground', elevationMm: 0 },
40	    { type: 'createLevel', id: 'seed-lvl-upper', name: 'Upper', elevationMm: 3000 },
41	    { type: 'createLevel', id: 'seed-lvl-roof-apex', name: 'Roof Apex', elevationMm: 5800 },
42	  );
43	
44	  // ── 2. Site + terrace (1 site, terrace pad as a -50 mm slab) ────────────
45	  cmds.push({
46	    type: 'upsertSite',
47	    id: 'seed-site',
48	    name: 'Site pad',
49	    referenceLevelId: 'seed-lvl-ground',
50	    boundaryMm: [
51	      { xMm: -10000, yMm: -8000 },
52	      { xMm: 25000, yMm: -8000 },
53	      { xMm: 25000, yMm: 18000 },
54	      { xMm: -10000, yMm: 18000 },
55	    ],
56	    padThicknessMm: 80,
57	    baseOffsetMm: -50,
58	    northDegCwFromPlanX: 0,
59	    uniformSetbackMm: 3000,
60	  });
61	
62	  // ── 3. Walls — exterior ground (6, L-shape) ─────────────────────────────
63	  const EXTERIOR_THICKNESS_MM = 200;
64	  const WALL_HEIGHT_MM = 2800;
65	  cmds.push(
66	    {
67	      type: 'createWall',
68	      id: 'seed-w-eg-south-main',
69	      name: 'EG South facade (main)',
70	      levelId: 'seed-lvl-ground',
71	      start: { xMm: 5000, yMm: 4000 },
72	      end: { xMm: 17000, yMm: 4000 },
73	      thicknessMm: EXTERIOR_THICKNESS_MM,
74	      heightMm: WALL_HEIGHT_MM,
75	    },
76	    {
77	      type: 'createWall',
78	      id: 'seed-w-eg-east-main',
79	      name: 'EG East facade (main)',
80	      levelId: 'seed-lvl-ground',
81	      start: { xMm: 17000, yMm: 4000 },
82	      end: { xMm: 17000, yMm: 8000 },
83	      thicknessMm: EXTERIOR_THICKNESS_MM,
84	      heightMm: WALL_HEIGHT_MM,
85	    },
86	    {
87	      type: 'createWall',
88	      id: 'seed-w-eg-south-wing',
89	      name: 'EG South facade (utility wing)',
90	      levelId: 'seed-lvl-ground',
91	      start: { xMm: 17000, yMm: 8000 },
92	      end: { xMm: 21000, yMm: 8000 },
93	      thicknessMm: EXTERIOR_THICKNESS_MM,
94	      heightMm: WALL_HEIGHT_MM,
95	    },
96	    {
97	      type: 'createWall',
98	      id: 'seed-w-eg-east-wing',
99	      name: 'EG East facade (utility wing)',
100	      levelId: 'seed-lvl-ground',
101	      start: { xMm: 21000, yMm: 8000 },
102	      end: { xMm: 21000, yMm: 12000 },
103	      thicknessMm: EXTERIOR_THICKNESS_MM,
104	      heightMm: WALL_HEIGHT_MM,
105	    },
106	    {
107	      type: 'createWall',
108	      id: 'seed-w-eg-north',
109	      name: 'EG North facade',
110	      levelId: 'seed-lvl-ground',
111	      start: { xMm: 21000, yMm: 12000 },
112	      end: { xMm: 5000, yMm: 12000 },
113	      thicknessMm: EXTERIOR_THICKNESS_MM,
114	      heightMm: WALL_HEIGHT_MM,
115	    },
116	    {
117	      type: 'createWall',
118	      id: 'seed-w-eg-west',
119	      name: 'EG West facade',
120	      levelId: 'seed-lvl-ground',
121	      start: { xMm: 5000, yMm: 12000 },
122	      end: { xMm: 5000, yMm: 4000 },
123	      thicknessMm: EXTERIOR_THICKNESS_MM,
124	      heightMm: WALL_HEIGHT_MM,
125	    },
126	  );
127	
128	  // ── 4. Walls — interior ground (4 partitions) ───────────────────────────
129	  const INTERIOR_THICKNESS_MM = 100;
130	  cmds.push(
131	    {
132	      type: 'createWall',
133	      id: 'seed-w-eg-spine',
134	      name: 'EG spine (entrance/living split)',
135	      levelId: 'seed-lvl-ground',
136	      start: { xMm: 11000, yMm: 4180 },
137	      end: { xMm: 11000, yMm: 12000 },
138	      thicknessMm: INTERIOR_THICKNESS_MM,
139	      heightMm: WALL_HEIGHT_MM,
140	    },
141	    {
142	      type: 'createWall',
143	      id: 'seed-w-eg-cross',
144	      name: 'EG kitchen/utility cross',
145	      levelId: 'seed-lvl-ground',
146	      start: { xMm: 11000, yMm: 8000 },
147	      end: { xMm: 17000, yMm: 8000 },
148	      thicknessMm: INTERIOR_THICKNESS_MM,
149	      heightMm: WALL_HEIGHT_MM,
150	    },
151	    {
152	      type: 'createWall',
153	      id: 'seed-w-eg-wc',
154	      name: 'EG WC partition',
155	      levelId: 'seed-lvl-ground',
156	      start: { xMm: 5180, yMm: 6000 },
157	      end: { xMm: 8000, yMm: 6000 },
158	      thicknessMm: INTERIOR_THICKNESS_MM,
159	      heightMm: WALL_HEIGHT_MM,
160	    },
161	    {
162	      type: 'createWall',
163	      id: 'seed-w-eg-utility',
164	      name: 'EG utility partition',
165	      levelId: 'seed-lvl-ground',
166	      start: { xMm: 17000, yMm: 10000 },
167	      end: { xMm: 21000, yMm: 10000 },
168	      thicknessMm: INTERIOR_THICKNESS_MM,
169	      heightMm: WALL_HEIGHT_MM,
170	    },
171	  );
172	
173	  // ── 5. Walls — exterior upper (4, set back 1 m on south for balcony) ────
174	  cmds.push(
175	    {
176	      type: 'createWall',
177	      id: 'seed-w-og-south',
178	      name: 'OG South facade (set back 1 m)',
179	      levelId: 'seed-lvl-upper',
180	      start: { xMm: 5000, yMm: 5000 },
181	      end: { xMm: 17000, yMm: 5000 },
182	      thicknessMm: EXTERIOR_THICKNESS_MM,
183	      heightMm: WALL_HEIGHT_MM,
184	    },
185	    {
186	      type: 'createWall',
187	      id: 'seed-w-og-east',
188	      name: 'OG East facade',
189	      levelId: 'seed-lvl-upper',
190	      start: { xMm: 17000, yMm: 5000 },
191	      end: { xMm: 17000, yMm: 12000 },
192	      thicknessMm: EXTERIOR_THICKNESS_MM,
193	      heightMm: WALL_HEIGHT_MM,
194	    },
195	    {
196	      type: 'createWall',
197	      id: 'seed-w-og-north',
198	      name: 'OG North facade',
199	      levelId: 'seed-lvl-upper',
200	      start: { xMm: 17000, yMm: 12000 },
201	      end: { xMm: 5000, yMm: 12000 },
202	      thicknessMm: EXTERIOR_THICKNESS_MM,
203	      heightMm: WALL_HEIGHT_MM,
204	    },
205	    {
206	      type: 'createWall',
207	      id: 'seed-w-og-west',
208	      name: 'OG West facade',
209	      levelId: 'seed-lvl-upper',
210	      start: { xMm: 5000, yMm: 12000 },
211	      end: { xMm: 5000, yMm: 5000 },
212	      thicknessMm: EXTERIOR_THICKNESS_MM,
213	      heightMm: WALL_HEIGHT_MM,
214	    },
215	  );
216	
217	  // ── 6. Walls — gable (2, north & south at 35° pitch, cladded) ──────────
218	  // Modelled as cladded walls riding above the upper-level eave height; the
219	  // pitched roof element drives the visible 35° geometry. The wall element
220	  // anchors the cladding hatch (vertical-board) called out in §27.1.
221	  const GABLE_HEIGHT_MM = 2000;
222	  cmds.push(
223	    {
224	      type: 'createWall',
225	      id: 'seed-w-gable-south',
226	      name: 'OG South gable (cladded, 35°)',
227	      levelId: 'seed-lvl-upper',
228	      start: { xMm: 5000, yMm: 5000 },
229	      end: { xMm: 17000, yMm: 5000 },
230	      thicknessMm: 150,
231	      heightMm: GABLE_HEIGHT_MM,
232	    },
233	    {
234	      type: 'createWall',
235	      id: 'seed-w-gable-north',
236	      name: 'OG North gable (cladded, 35°)',
237	      levelId: 'seed-lvl-upper',
238	      start: { xMm: 17000, yMm: 12000 },
239	      end: { xMm: 5000, yMm: 12000 },
240	      thicknessMm: 150,
241	      heightMm: GABLE_HEIGHT_MM,
242	    },
243	  );
244	
245	  // ── 7. Floors / slabs (3) ───────────────────────────────────────────────
246	  // Ground slab — full L-shape footprint.
247	  cmds.push({
248	    type: 'createFloor',
249	    id: 'seed-floor-eg',
250	    name: 'EG slab (L-shape)',
251	    levelId: 'seed-lvl-ground',
252	    boundaryMm: [
253	      { xMm: 5000, yMm: 4000 },
254	      { xMm: 17000, yMm: 4000 },
255	      { xMm: 17000, yMm: 8000 },
256	      { xMm: 21000, yMm: 8000 },
257	      { xMm: 21000, yMm: 12000 },
258	      { xMm: 5000, yMm: 12000 },
259	    ],
260	    thicknessMm: 220,
261	    structureThicknessMm: 140,
262	    finishThicknessMm: 30,
263	    roomBounded: true,
264	  });
265	
266	  // Upper slab — main rect including the 1 m south cantilever (balcony floor).
267	  cmds.push({
268	    type: 'createFloor',
269	    id: 'seed-floor-og',
270	    name: 'OG slab (with south cantilever)',
271	    levelId: 'seed-lvl-upper',
272	    boundaryMm: [
273	      { xMm: 5000, yMm: 4000 },
274	      { xMm: 17000, yMm: 4000 },
275	      { xMm: 17000, yMm: 12000 },
276	      { xMm: 5000, yMm: 12000 },
277	    ],
278	    thicknessMm: 240,
279	    structureThicknessMm: 160,
280	    finishThicknessMm: 30,
281	    roomBounded: true,
282	  });
283	
284	  // Terrace slab — 12 m × 9 m at -50 mm, south of main.
285	  cmds.push({
286	    type: 'createFloor',

file.content:
def try_commit_bundle(
    doc: Document,
    cmds_raw: list[dict[str, Any]],
) -> tuple[bool, Document | None, list[Command], list[Violation], str]:
    cmds: list[Command] = [coerce_command(c) for c in cmds_raw]
    cand = clone_document(doc)
    for cmd in cmds:
        apply_inplace(cand, cmd)

    violations = evaluate(cand.elements)

    blocking = [v for v in violations if v.blocking or v.severity == "error"]
    if blocking:
        return False, None, cmds, violations, "constraint_error"

    cand.revision = doc.revision + 1

    return True, cand, cmds, violations, "ok"


def try_commit(
    doc: Document, cmd_raw: dict[str, Any]
) -> tuple[bool, Document | None, Command, list[Violation], str]:
    cmds = coerce_command(cmd_raw)
    cand = clone_document(doc)
    apply_inplace(cand, cmds)

    violations = evaluate(cand.elements)

    blocking = [v for v in violations if v.blocking or v.severity == "error"]
    if blocking:
        return False, None, cmds, violations, "constraint_error"

    cand.revision = doc.revision + 1

    return True, cand, cmds, violations, "ok"

class FloorElem(BaseModel):
    model_config = ConfigDict(extra="ignore", populate_by_name=True)
    kind: Literal["floor"] = "floor"
    id: str
    name: str = "Floor"
    level_id: str = Field(alias="levelId")
    boundary_mm: list[Vec2Mm] = Field(alias="boundaryMm")
    thickness_mm: float = Field(alias="thicknessMm", default=220)
    structure_thickness_mm: float = Field(alias="structureThicknessMm", default=140)
    finish_thickness_mm: float = Field(alias="finishThicknessMm", default=0)
    floor_type_id: str | None = Field(default=None, alias="floorTypeId")
    insulation_extension_mm: float = Field(default=0, alias="insulationExtensionMm")
    room_bounded: bool = Field(default=False, alias="roomBounded")


class RoofElem(BaseModel):
    model_config = ConfigDict(extra="ignore", populate_by_name=True)
    kind: Literal["roof"] = "roof"
    id: str
    name: str = "Roof"
    reference_level_id: str = Field(alias="referenceLevelId")
    footprint_mm: list[Vec2Mm] = Field(alias="footprintMm")
    overhang_mm: float = Field(default=400, alias="overhangMm")
    slope_deg: float | None = Field(default=25.0, alias="slopeDeg")
    edge_slope_flags: dict[str, bool] = Field(default_factory=dict, alias="edgeSlopeFlags")
    roof_geometry_mode: RoofGeometryMode = Field(default="mass_box", alias="roofGeometryMode")
    roof_type_id: str | None = Field(default=None, alias="roofTypeId")


class StairElem(BaseModel):
    model_config = ConfigDict(extra="ignore", populate_by_name=True)
    kind: Literal["stair"] = "stair"
    id: str
    name: str = "Stair"
    base_level_id: str = Field(alias="baseLevelId")
    top_level_id: str = Field(alias="topLevelId")
    run_start: Vec2Mm = Field(alias="runStartMm")
    run_end: Vec2Mm = Field(alias="runEndMm")
    width_mm: float = Field(alias="widthMm", default=1000)
    riser_mm: float = Field(alias="riserMm", default=175)
    tread_mm: float = Field(alias="treadMm", default=275)


class SlabOpeningElem(BaseModel):
    model_config = ConfigDict(extra="ignore", populate_by_name=True)
    kind: Literal["slab_opening"] = "slab_opening"
    id: str
    name: str = "Opening"
    host_floor_id: str = Field(alias="hostFloorId")
    boundary_mm: list[Vec2Mm] = Field(alias="boundaryMm")
    is_shaft: bool = Field(default=False, alias="isShaft")


class RailingElem(BaseModel):
    model_config = ConfigDict(extra="ignore", populate_by_name=True)
    kind: Literal["railing"] = "railing"
    id: str
    name: str = "Railing"
    hosted_stair_id: str | None = Field(default=None, alias="hostedStairId")
    path_mm: list[Vec2Mm] = Field(alias="pathMm")
    guard_height_mm: float = Field(alias="guardHeightMm", default=1040)


class FamilyTypeElem(BaseModel):
    model_config = ConfigDict(extra="ignore", populate_by_name=True)
    kind: Literal["family_type"] = "family_type"
    id: str
    discipline: Literal["door", "window", "generic"] = "generic"
    parameters: dict[str, Any] = Field(default_factory=dict)


class RoomSeparationElem(BaseModel):
    model_config = ConfigDict(extra="ignore", populate_by_name=True)
    kind: Literal["room_separation"] = "room_separation"
    id: str
    name: str = "Room separator"
    level_id: str = Field(alias="levelId")
    start: Vec2Mm
    end: Vec2Mm


class PlanRegionElem(BaseModel):
    model_config = ConfigDict(extra="ignore", populate_by_name=True)
    kind: Literal["plan_region"] = "plan_region"
    id: str
    name: str = "Plan region"
    level_id: str = Field(alias="levelId")
    outline_mm: list[Vec2Mm] = Field(alias="outlineMm")
    cut_plane_offset_mm: float = Field(alias="cutPlaneOffsetMm", default=-500)


class TagDefinitionElem(BaseModel):
    model_config = ConfigDict(extra="ignore", populate_by_name=True)
    kind: Literal["tag_definition"] = "tag_definition"
    id: str
    name: str = "Tag"
    tag_kind: Literal["room", "sill", "slab_finish", "custom"] = Field(
        default="custom", alias="tagKind"
    )
    discipline: str = Field(default="architecture")

class Document(BaseModel):
    revision: int = 1
    elements: dict[str, Annotated[Element, Field(discriminator="kind")]]

#!/usr/bin/env python3
from __future__ import annotations

import asyncio
import uuid
from datetime import UTC, datetime

from bim_ai.db import SessionMaker, init_db_schema
from bim_ai.document import Document
from bim_ai.elements import (
    CameraMm,
    DimensionElem,
    DoorElem,
    Element,
    GridLineElem,
    IssueElem,
    LevelElem,
    RoomElem,
    Vec2Mm,
    Vec3Mm,
    ViewpointElem,
    WallElem,
    WindowElem,
)
from bim_ai.engine import try_commit_bundle
from bim_ai.tables import CommentRecord, ModelRecord, ProjectRecord

PROJECT_ID = uuid.uuid5(uuid.NAMESPACE_URL, "bim-ai:project:demo")
MODEL_ID = uuid.uuid5(uuid.NAMESPACE_URL, "bim-ai:model:demo-main")
COMMENT_ID = uuid.uuid5(uuid.NAMESPACE_URL, "bim-ai:comment:demo-1")


def demo_document() -> Document:
    wall_spine = "wall-001"
    wall_ns = "wall-002"

    elems: dict[str, Element] = {
        "lvl-1": LevelElem(kind="level", id="lvl-1", name="Ground", elevationMm=0),
        "lvl-2": LevelElem(kind="level", id="lvl-2", name="Upper", elevationMm=3000),
        wall_spine: WallElem(
            kind="wall",
            id=wall_spine,
            name="Spine wall",
            levelId="lvl-1",
            start=Vec2Mm(xMm=0, yMm=0),
            end=Vec2Mm(xMm=14000, yMm=0),
            thicknessMm=200,
            heightMm=2800,
        ),
        wall_ns: WallElem(
            kind="wall",
            id=wall_ns,
            name="North façade",
            levelId="lvl-1",
            start=Vec2Mm(xMm=0, yMm=-4000),
            end=Vec2Mm(xMm=14000, yMm=-4000),
            thicknessMm=200,
            heightMm=2800,
        ),
        "wall-020": WallElem(
            kind="wall",
            id="wall-020",
            name="West end",
            levelId="lvl-1",
            start=Vec2Mm(xMm=0, yMm=-4000),
            end=Vec2Mm(xMm=0, yMm=2000),
            thicknessMm=200,
            heightMm=2800,
        ),
        "wall-up-1": WallElem(
            kind="wall",
            id="wall-up-1",
            name="Upper spine",
            levelId="lvl-2",
            start=Vec2Mm(xMm=2000, yMm=-2000),
            end=Vec2Mm(xMm=9000, yMm=-2000),
            thicknessMm=200,
            heightMm=2800,
        ),
        "door-001": DoorElem(
            kind="door",
            id="door-001",
            name="Entry",
            wallId=wall_spine,

def document_wire(doc: Document) -> dict:
    return {
        "revision": doc.revision,
        "elements": {kid: elem.model_dump(by_alias=True) for kid, elem in doc.elements.items()},
    }


async def seed_async() -> None:
    await init_db_schema()
    demo = demo_document()
    ok_bundle, demo2, _, _, _code = try_commit_bundle(
        demo,
        [
            {
                "type": "createRoomRectangle",
                "levelId": "lvl-1",
                "origin": {"xMm": 15500, "yMm": -5500},
                "widthMm": 2600,
                "depthMm": 1900,
            }
        ],
    )
    if ok_bundle:
        demo = demo2
    demo_wire = document_wire(demo)
    now = datetime.now(UTC)

    async with SessionMaker() as session:
        if await session.get(ProjectRecord, PROJECT_ID) is None:
            session.add(ProjectRecord(id=PROJECT_ID, slug="demo", title="BIM AI demo project"))

        row = await session.get(ModelRecord, MODEL_ID)
        if row is None:
            session.add(
                ModelRecord(
                    id=MODEL_ID,
                    project_id=PROJECT_ID,
                    slug="main",
                    revision=demo.revision,
                    document=demo_wire,
                ),
            )
        else:
            row.document = demo_wire
            row.revision = demo.revision
            row.slug = "main"
            row.project_id = PROJECT_ID

        crowd = await session.get(CommentRecord, COMMENT_ID)
        if crowd is None:
            session.add(
                CommentRecord(
                    id=COMMENT_ID,
                    model_id=MODEL_ID,
                    user_display="Seed bot",
                    body="Try Plan ( toolbar ) · Snap + tools: Wall / Door / Window / Room / Grid / Dimension.",
                    element_id=None,
                    level_id="lvl-1",
                    anchor_x_mm=6200,
                    anchor_y_mm=-2800,
                    resolved=False,
                    created_at=now,
                    updated_at=now,
                ),
            )

        await session.commit()

    print("seed: OK — demo/main model:", str(MODEL_ID))


def main() -> None:
    asyncio.run(seed_async())


if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()

input.prompt:
I'm investigating why two elements are not visually rendering in a BIM AI app at /Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai:

1. **Roof not visible in 3D** — A `roof` element exists in the database (roofGeometryMode="mass_box", slopeDeg=30, overhangMm=500) but doesn't appear in the 3D view. When running `try_commit_bundle` the engine emits these info violations:
   - `bim_ai_gable_roof_v0 not emitted... skipReasonCode='no_eligible_elements'`
   - `bim_ai_roof_unsupported_shape_summary_v0 not emitted... skipReasonCode='no_eligible_elements'`
   - `bim_ai_roof_layered_prism_witness_v1 not emitted... skipReasonCode='no_eligible_elements'`

2. **Section cut not rendering content** — A `section_cut` element exists but the section view tab appears empty.

Please search for:
- How the glTF/3D export works for `roof` elements — what makes a roof "eligible" for the extensions, what geometry mode is needed, what conditions must be met
- Whether there's a known limitation/TODO for roof rendering  
- How `section_cut` elements are rendered in the section view — is there a renderer, a separate pipeline?
- Search files: gltf, renderer, export, roof, section_cut in the frontend packages and backend

Report the key findings: what conditions must a roof element meet to render, and what makes section cuts visible. Search breadth: very thorough.

content.text:
Perfect! Now I have a comprehensive understanding. Let me create a final summary document based on my findings.

## FINDINGS REPORT: Roof Rendering & Section Cut Visibility in BIM AI App

Based on my thorough investigation of the BIM AI codebase, I've identified the key conditions and mechanisms for both roof rendering in 3D and section cut visibility.

---

### **ROOF RENDERING CONDITIONS (3D View)**

#### **Why the Roof with `roofGeometryMode="mass_box"` Doesn't Render in glTF**

The three extension tokens you're seeing as "not emitted" are all conditional on the roof meeting **specific geometric and metadata criteria**:

1. **`bim_ai_gable_roof_v0` — REQUIRES:**
   - Footprint must be exactly **4 axis-aligned rectangle corners** (with 1 mm tolerance per corner)
   - `roofGeometryMode` must be `"gable_pitched_rectangle"` (NOT `"mass_box"`)
   - `slope_deg` must be defined and non-None
   - `reference_level_id` must resolve to a valid `LevelElem`
   - The function `gable_pitched_rectangle_elevation_supported_v0()` must return `True`

2. **`bim_ai_roof_geometry_evidence_v1` — REQUIRES:**
   - Roof must have at least 3 footprint vertices (non-degenerate)
   - Reference level must resolve
   - Slope must be defined
   - This extension emits evidence even for ineligible roofs (with deferred tokens like `"non_rectangular_footprint_deferred"`)

3. **`bim_ai_roof_layered_prism_witness_v1` — REQUIRES:**
   - Must be `"gable_pitched_rectangle_supported"` (the strict condition)
   - AND have a valid `roofTypeId` linking to a material layer stack

**⚠️ KEY FINDING:** Your roof with `roofGeometryMode="mass_box"` **cannot emit `bim_ai_gable_roof_v0`** because that extension is only for gable roofs. The `mass_box` mode is a placeholder that shows a peak-point proxy in sections, not a pitched roof geometry.

#### **How the Backend Determines "Eligible" Roofs**

File: `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/app/bim_ai/export_gltf.py` lines 233–843

The function `_document_has_gable_roof_mesh()` filters roofs via `_collect_gable_roof_visual_slices()`:

```python
def _collect_gable_roof_visual_slices(doc: Document) -> list[_GableRoofVisual]:
    out: list[_GableRoofVisual] = []
    for rid in sorted(eid for eid, e in doc.elements.items() if isinstance(e, RoofElem)):
        rf = doc.elements[rid]
        # Only include if gable_pitched_rectangle_elevation_supported_v0 returns True
        if not gable_pitched_rectangle_elevation_supported_v0(
            footprint_mm=pts,
            roof_geometry_mode=rf.roof_geometry_mode,
            reference_level_resolves=isinstance(doc.elements.get(rf.reference_level_id), LevelElem),
            slope_deg=rf.slope_deg,
        ):
            continue
        # ... process the roof into _GableRoofVisual
    return out
```

**Only roofs passing this check are added to the glTF mesh export with `+bim_ai_gable_roof_v0`.**

#### **The `roof_geometry_support_token_v0()` Decision Matrix**

File: `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/app/bim_ai/roof_geometry.py` lines 145–178

This function returns **why** a roof isn't eligible:

| Condition | Returns | Meaning |
|-----------|---------|---------|
| Missing slope, level, or <3 vertices | `"missing_slope_or_level"` | Cannot determine geometry |
| Degenerate footprint (area ≈ 0) | `"non_rectangular_footprint_deferred"` | Footprint collapses to a line/point |
| Concave footprint (L-shaped, etc.) | `"valley_candidate_deferred"` | Hip/valley roofs — deferred (not supported yet) |
| `roofGeometryMode="gable_pitched_rectangle"` + 4 axis-aligned corners | `"gable_pitched_rectangle_supported"` | ✅ **ELIGIBLE FOR MESH** |
| Convex polygon (not rect) with 4+ corners | `"hip_candidate_deferred"` | Hip roofs — deferred |
| `roofGeometryMode="mass_box"` + rect + convex | `None` | ✅ **ELIGIBLE FOR MASS_BOX**, NOT gable mesh |
| Anything else | `"non_rectangular_footprint_deferred"` | Non-rectangular roofs deferred |

**Your roof returns `None` (mass_box eligible) but the glTF exporter only emits `bim_ai_gable_roof_v0` for `"gable_pitched_rectangle_supported"` roofs.**

---

### **SECTION CUT RENDERING**

#### **How Section Cuts Get Content**

Files:
- Backend: `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/app/bim_ai/section_projection_primitives.py` lines 1046–1150
- Backend: `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/app/bim_ai/plan_projection_wire.py` lines 2217–2262
- Frontend: `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/packages/web/src/plan/sectionProjectionWire.ts`
- Frontend: `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/packages/web/src/workspace/sectionViewportSvg.tsx`

**The pipeline:**

1. **Backend** calls `build_section_projection_primitives(doc, section_cut_elem)` which:
   - Extracts walls, floors, doors, windows, stairs, and **roofs** that intersect the cut plane
   - For each roof, calculates the `u` (along-cut) and `z` (elevation) spans
   - Returns primitives with roof rows containing: `uStartMm`, `uEndMm`, `zMidMm`, `proxyKind` (`"gablePitchedRectangleChord"` or `"footprintChord"`)

2. **Frontend** fetches via `GET /api/models/{modelId}/projection/section/{sectionCutId}`
   - Receives the complete primitives JSON including `"roofs": [...]`
   - Renders SVG/canvas geometry for each roof in the section view

#### **Roof Projection in Sections — NO GEOMETRY MODE REQUIREMENT**

**Critical difference:** Section roofs are **always rendered** if they intersect the cut plane, **regardless of geometry mode**:

```python
# section_projection_primitives.py:1079–1150
if gable_ok:  # gable_pitched_rectangle_supported
    # Render with ridge, eave, and fascia edges
    gable_row = {
        "proxyKind": "gablePitchedRectangleChord",
        "ridgeZMm": ridge_z,
        "ridgeAxisPlan": ridge_axis,
        # ... full pitch geometry
    }
    roofs.append(gable_row)
else:
    # Render footprint proxy with peak point (for mass_box or non-rectangular)
    z_mid = _roof_proxy_top_z_mm(doc, e)  # Uses slope to compute peak
    fp_row = {
        "proxyKind": "footprintChord",
        "zMidMm": z_mid,  # Peak height computed from slope
        # ... footprint outline only
    }
    roofs.append(fp_row)
```

**Both paths append roofs to the section primitives.** If your section cut is returning empty roofs, check:

1. **Is the roof footprint inside the crop band?** The function `_polygon_u_span_in_strip()` clips the roof polygon against the perpendicular crop depth. If the roof is outside `cropDepthMm / 2` from the cut line, it won't appear.

2. **Is the `reference_level_id` valid?** The code still validates level resolution; if it fails, the roof is skipped.

3. **Is the footprint degenerate?** A roof with <3 vertices or collapsed area is skipped.

---

### **SUMMARY TABLE: When Roofs Are Visible**

| View | Geometry Mode | Footprint Shape | Conditions Met | Result |
|------|---------------|-----------------|----------------|--------|
| **3D glTF mesh** | `gable_pitched_rectangle` | 4 axis-aligned rect | Level OK, slope OK | ✅ `bim_ai_gable_roof_v0` emitted, 3D ridge + slopes rendered |
| **3D glTF mesh** | `mass_box` | 4 axis-aligned rect | Level OK, slope OK | ❌ No mesh (mass_box uses proxy, not geometric) |
| **3D glTF mesh** | Any | Non-rectangular | N/A | ❌ Deferred (hip/valley/concave) |
| **Section view** | `gable_pitched_rectangle` | 4 axis-aligned rect | Intersects cut crop band | ✅ Gable chord with ridge |
| **Section view** | `mass_box` | Any valid | Intersects cut crop band | ✅ Footprint chord with peak point |
| **Section view** | Any | Non-rectangular | Intersects cut crop band | ✅ Footprint chord with peak point |

---

### **ROOT CAUSES FOR YOUR TWO ISSUES**

**1. Roof not visible in 3D:**
- Your roof has `roofGeometryMode="mass_box"` — this mode has **no glTF mesh export**. The `mass_box` mode is a placeholder for section-view-only geometry that shows a simple peak point.
- To render a roof in 3D, change `roofGeometryMode` to `"gable_pitched_rectangle"` and ensure the footprint is exactly 4 axis-aligned rectangle corners.

**2. Section cut not rendering content (if true):**
- The roof *should* appear in sections if it intersects the cut band, **even in mass_box mode**.
- Check if: (a) the roof footprint falls outside the `cropDepthMm` perp band, (b) the roof has <3 vertices, (c) the `reference_level_id` doesn't resolve, (d) the API endpoint is returning empty roofs in the primitives JSON.

---

### **FILES TO REFERENCE**

**Backend geometry logic:**
- `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/app/bim_ai/roof_geometry.py` — decision matrix
- `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/app/bim_ai/export_gltf.py` — glTF mesh eligibility (lines 233–843, 1046–1150)
- `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/app/bim_ai/section_projection_primitives.py` — section roof projection (lines 1046–1150)

**Tests (for validation):**
- `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/app/tests/test_roof_pitch_evidence.py` — roof extension conditions
- `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/app/tests/test_plan_projection_and_evidence_slices.py` — section roof rendering

**Frontend rendering:**
- `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/packages/web/src/workspace/sectionViewportSvg.tsx` — section SVG renderer

   - `bim_ai_gable_roof_v0 not emitted... skipReasonCode='no_eligible_elements'`
   - `bim_ai_roof_unsupported_shape_summary_v0 not emitted... skipReasonCode='no_eligible_elements'`

2. **Section cut not rendering content** — A `section_cut` element exists but the section view tab appears empty.

Please search for:

content.text:
Perfect! Now I have a comprehensive understanding. Let me create a final summary document based on my findings.

## FINDINGS REPORT: Roof Rendering & Section Cut Visibility in BIM AI App

Based on my thorough investigation of the BIM AI codebase, I've identified the key conditions and mechanisms for both roof rendering in 3D and section cut visibility.

---

### **ROOF RENDERING CONDITIONS (3D View)**

#### **Why the Roof with `roofGeometryMode="mass_box"` Doesn't Render in glTF**

The three extension tokens you're seeing as "not emitted" are all conditional on the roof meeting **specific geometric and metadata criteria**:

1. **`bim_ai_gable_roof_v0` — REQUIRES:**
   - Footprint must be exactly **4 axis-aligned rectangle corners** (with 1 mm tolerance per corner)
   - `roofGeometryMode` must be `"gable_pitched_rectangle"` (NOT `"mass_box"`)
   - `slope_deg` must be defined and non-None
   - `reference_level_id` must resolve to a valid `LevelElem`
   - The function `gable_pitched_rectangle_elevation_supported_v0()` must return `True`

2. **`bim_ai_roof_geometry_evidence_v1` — REQUIRES:**
   - Roof must have at least 3 footprint vertices (non-degenerate)
   - Reference level must resolve
   - Slope must be defined
   - This extension emits evidence even for ineligible roofs (with deferred tokens like `"non_rectangular_footprint_deferred"`)

3. **`bim_ai_roof_layered_prism_witness_v1` — REQUIRES:**
   - Must be `"gable_pitched_rectangle_supported"` (the strict condition)
   - AND have a valid `roofTypeId` linking to a material layer stack

**⚠️ KEY FINDING:** Your roof with `roofGeometryMode="mass_box"` **cannot emit `bim_ai_gable_roof_v0`** because that extension is only for gable roofs. The `mass_box` mode is a placeholder that shows a peak-point proxy in sections, not a pitched roof geometry.

#### **How the Backend Determines "Eligible" Roofs**

File: `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/app/bim_ai/export_gltf.py` lines 233–843

The function `_document_has_gable_roof_mesh()` filters roofs via `_collect_gable_roof_visual_slices()`:

```python
def _collect_gable_roof_visual_slices(doc: Document) -> list[_GableRoofVisual]:
    out: list[_GableRoofVisual] = []
    for rid in sorted(eid for eid, e in doc.elements.items() if isinstance(e, RoofElem)):
        rf = doc.elements[rid]
        # Only include if gable_pitched_rectangle_elevation_supported_v0 returns True
        if not gable_pitched_rectangle_elevation_supported_v0(
            footprint_mm=pts,
            roof_geometry_mode=rf.roof_geometry_mode,
            reference_level_resolves=isinstance(doc.elements.get(rf.reference_level_id), LevelElem),
            slope_deg=rf.slope_deg,
        ):
            continue
        # ... process the roof into _GableRoofVisual
    return out
```

**Only roofs passing this check are added to the glTF mesh export with `+bim_ai_gable_roof_v0`.**

#### **The `roof_geometry_support_token_v0()` Decision Matrix**

File: `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/app/bim_ai/roof_geometry.py` lines 145–178

This function returns **why** a roof isn't eligible:

| Condition | Returns | Meaning |
|-----------|---------|---------|
| Missing slope, level, or <3 vertices | `"missing_slope_or_level"` | Cannot determine geometry |
| Degenerate footprint (area ≈ 0) | `"non_rectangular_footprint_deferred"` | Footprint collapses to a line/point |
| Concave footprint (L-shaped, etc.) | `"valley_candidate_deferred"` | Hip/valley roofs — deferred (not supported yet) |
| `roofGeometryMode="gable_pitched_rectangle"` + 4 axis-aligned corners | `"gable_pitched_rectangle_supported"` | ✅ **ELIGIBLE FOR MESH** |
| Convex polygon (not rect) with 4+ corners | `"hip_candidate_deferred"` | Hip roofs — deferred |
| `roofGeometryMode="mass_box"` + rect + convex | `None` | ✅ **ELIGIBLE FOR MASS_BOX**, NOT gable mesh |
| Anything else | `"non_rectangular_footprint_deferred"` | Non-rectangular roofs deferred |

**Your roof returns `None` (mass_box eligible) but the glTF exporter only emits `bim_ai_gable_roof_v0` for `"gable_pitched_rectangle_supported"` roofs.**

---

### **SECTION CUT RENDERING**

#### **How Section Cuts Get Content**

Files:
- Backend: `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/app/bim_ai/section_projection_primitives.py` lines 1046–1150
- Backend: `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/app/bim_ai/plan_projection_wire.py` lines 2217–2262
- Frontend: `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/packages/web/src/plan/sectionProjectionWire.ts`
- Frontend: `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/packages/web/src/workspace/sectionViewportSvg.tsx`

**The pipeline:**

1. **Backend** calls `build_section_projection_primitives(doc, section_cut_elem)` which:
   - Extracts walls, floors, doors, windows, stairs, and **roofs** that intersect the cut plane
   - For each roof, calculates the `u` (along-cut) and `z` (elevation) spans
   - Returns primitives with roof rows containing: `uStartMm`, `uEndMm`, `zMidMm`, `proxyKind` (`"gablePitchedRectangleChord"` or `"footprintChord"`)

2. **Frontend** fetches via `GET /api/models/{modelId}/projection/section/{sectionCutId}`
   - Receives the complete primitives JSON including `"roofs": [...]`
   - Renders SVG/canvas geometry for each roof in the section view

#### **Roof Projection in Sections — NO GEOMETRY MODE REQUIREMENT**

**Critical difference:** Section roofs are **always rendered** if they intersect the cut plane, **regardless of geometry mode**:

```python
# section_projection_primitives.py:1079–1150
if gable_ok:  # gable_pitched_rectangle_supported
    # Render with ridge, eave, and fascia edges
    gable_row = {
        "proxyKind": "gablePitchedRectangleChord",
        "ridgeZMm": ridge_z,
        "ridgeAxisPlan": ridge_axis,
        # ... full pitch geometry
    }
    roofs.append(gable_row)
else:
    # Render footprint proxy with peak point (for mass_box or non-rectangular)
    z_mid = _roof_proxy_top_z_mm(doc, e)  # Uses slope to compute peak
    fp_row = {
        "proxyKind": "footprintChord",
        "zMidMm": z_mid,  # Peak height computed from slope
        # ... footprint outline only
    }
    roofs.append(fp_row)
```

**Both paths append roofs to the section primitives.** If your section cut is returning empty roofs, check:

1. **Is the roof footprint inside the crop band?** The function `_polygon_u_span_in_strip()` clips the roof polygon against the perpendicular crop depth. If the roof is outside `cropDepthMm / 2` from the cut line, it won't appear.

2. **Is the `reference_level_id` valid?** The code still validates level resolution; if it fails, the roof is skipped.

3. **Is the footprint degenerate?** A roof with <3 vertices or collapsed area is skipped.

---

### **SUMMARY TABLE: When Roofs Are Visible**

| View | Geometry Mode | Footprint Shape | Conditions Met | Result |
|------|---------------|-----------------|----------------|--------|
| **3D glTF mesh** | `gable_pitched_rectangle` | 4 axis-aligned rect | Level OK, slope OK | ✅ `bim_ai_gable_roof_v0` emitted, 3D ridge + slopes rendered |
| **3D glTF mesh** | `mass_box` | 4 axis-aligned rect | Level OK, slope OK | ❌ No mesh (mass_box uses proxy, not geometric) |
| **3D glTF mesh** | Any | Non-rectangular | N/A | ❌ Deferred (hip/valley/concave) |
| **Section view** | `gable_pitched_rectangle` | 4 axis-aligned rect | Intersects cut crop band | ✅ Gable chord with ridge |
| **Section view** | `mass_box` | Any valid | Intersects cut crop band | ✅ Footprint chord with peak point |
| **Section view** | Any | Non-rectangular | Intersects cut crop band | ✅ Footprint chord with peak point |

---

### **ROOT CAUSES FOR YOUR TWO ISSUES**

**1. Roof not visible in 3D:**
- Your roof has `roofGeometryMode="mass_box"` — this mode has **no glTF mesh export**. The `mass_box` mode is a placeholder for section-view-only geometry that shows a simple peak point.
- To render a roof in 3D, change `roofGeometryMode` to `"gable_pitched_rectangle"` and ensure the footprint is exactly 4 axis-aligned rectangle corners.

**2. Section cut not rendering content (if true):**
- The roof *should* appear in sections if it intersects the cut band, **even in mass_box mode**.
- Check if: (a) the roof footprint falls outside the `cropDepthMm` perp band, (b) the roof has <3 vertices, (c) the `reference_level_id` doesn't resolve, (d) the API endpoint is returning empty roofs in the primitives JSON.

---

### **FILES TO REFERENCE**

**Backend geometry logic:**
- `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/app/bim_ai/roof_geometry.py` — decision matrix
- `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/app/bim_ai/export_gltf.py` — glTF mesh eligibility (lines 233–843, 1046–1150)
- `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/app/bim_ai/section_projection_primitives.py` — section roof projection (lines 1046–1150)

**Tests (for validation):**
- `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/app/tests/test_roof_pitch_evidence.py` — roof extension conditions
- `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/app/tests/test_plan_projection_and_evidence_slices.py` — section roof rendering

**Frontend rendering:**
- `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/packages/web/src/workspace/sectionViewportSvg.tsx` — section SVG renderer

input.content:
# Element Rendering Requirements

Reference for which element properties are required for each element to be
**visually present** in the 3D view, plan view, and section view. Gaps here
are the most common cause of "I added element X but it doesn't appear".

---

## Roof

### 3D view (glTF mesh)

The glTF exporter only emits a pitched roof mesh when **all** of the following
hold. Any miss causes the roof to be silently omitted from the 3D scene.

| Requirement | Value needed | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| `roofGeometryMode` | `"gable_pitched_rectangle"` | Default `"mass_box"` produces **no mesh** — it is a plan/section proxy only |
| Footprint shape | Exactly 4 axis-aligned rectangle corners (≤1 mm tolerance) | L-shapes, hip polygons → deferred, no 3D mesh yet |
| `slopeDeg` | non-null float | If `None`, roof is skipped |
| `referenceLevelId` | resolves to a `LevelElem` | Broken ref → skipped |

Extension emitted when eligible: `bim_ai_gable_roof_v0`

**Unsupported footprint shapes in 3D (deferred):**
- Concave / L-shaped → `"valley_candidate_deferred"`
- Convex polygon with >4 corners (hip) → `"hip_candidate_deferred"`

### Section view

Roofs appear in section regardless of geometry mode, as long as the footprint
intersects the section cut's crop band (`cropDepthMm / 2` on each side of the
cut line).

- `roofGeometryMode = "gable_pitched_rectangle"` → renders with ridge line +
  sloped eave edges (`proxyKind: "gablePitchedRectangleChord"`)
- Any other mode → renders a flat footprint chord with a peak point
  (`proxyKind: "footprintChord"`)

**Key code:** `app/bim_ai/section_projection_primitives.py` —
`build_section_projection_primitives()`  
**Geometry decision matrix:** `app/bim_ai/roof_geometry.py` —
`roof_geometry_support_token_v0()`

---

## Section cut

A `section_cut` element creates a section tab in the UI. The section view
renders by calling `GET /api/models/{id}/projection/section/{sectionCutId}`.

For content to appear:

| Requirement | Notes |
|---|---|
| Section line must intersect elements | Elements must fall within `cropDepthMm / 2` of the cut line (perp distance) |
| `lineStartMm` / `lineEndMm` span must cover the building footprint | Short cut lines leave out geometry |
| `cropDepthMm` large enough | For a full-building section, use ≥ building depth + some margin |

The section marker/symbol (dashed line + arrows) renders on the plan canvas
automatically when a `section_cut` element exists on that level's plan.

---

## Wall

Walls render in all views unconditionally if `levelId` resolves and
`start ≠ end`. No extra conditions.

`thicknessMm` affects plan hatch rendering. Very thin walls (< ~50 mm) may
not be distinguishable in small-scale plans.

---

## Floor / Slab

Floors render in 3D and plan unconditionally if `levelId` resolves and
`boundaryMm` has ≥ 3 non-degenerate vertices.

`roomBounded: true` causes the floor to visually clip to the room boundary
instead of the explicit polygon — only use when rooms cover the full floor area.

---

## Stair

Stairs render in 3D and plan if `baseLevelId` and `topLevelId` both resolve
and `runStartMm ≠ runEndMm`. The stair shaft must have a matching
`slab_opening` on the `hostFloorId` of the upper-level floor, otherwise the
upper slab visually occludes the stair run.

---

## Door / Window

Openings render only if their `wallId` resolves to an existing wall. The
`alongT` value (0–1 fraction along the wall) must place the full opening
within the wall extent — openings that stray outside the wall are silently
clamped or dropped.

---

## Railing

Railings render in 3D if:
- `hostedStairId` resolves (stair-hosted railing), OR
- `pathMm` has ≥ 2 points (free-standing railing)

---

## Room

Rooms render as coloured fills on the plan canvas. They do **not** produce
geometry in the 3D view (they're semantic, not geometric). `outlineMm` must
have ≥ 3 non-degenerate vertices.

---

## Viewpoint

A `saveViewpoint` command stores a named camera position. The viewpoint with
`id = "vp-001"` is loaded as the default camera when the 3D tab is first
opened. All viewpoints appear in the sidebar under **3D Views**.

---

## Grid line

Grid lines render on the plan canvas only. They have no 3D representation.

---

## Dimension

Dimensions render on the plan canvas only. `aMm` and `bMm` define the two
measurement endpoints; `offsetMm` shifts the dimension line perpendicular to
the measured direction.

---

## Summary checklist for a complete seed / demo model

- [ ] Roof: `roofGeometryMode: "gable_pitched_rectangle"` + 4-corner axis-aligned footprint + `slopeDeg` set
- [ ] Stair: has matching `slab_opening` with `isShaft: true` on the floor above
- [ ] Section cut: `cropDepthMm` ≥ building depth; line spans the full footprint
- [ ] Floors: `roomBounded` only when rooms tile the full area without gaps
- [ ] Viewpoint `vp-001` exists for the default 3D camera position
